title: Today's Tech Brief: AI Porn and Easy ID
summary: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
imageAlt: Image from source article for FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
imageCaption: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
imageSourceTitle: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent

body.text: I'm looking at a recent case that highlights the growing issue of AI-generated pornography being shared without consent. An FBI agent has explained how easy it is to identify individuals posting this content, thanks to a simple yet telling mistake. This case is part of a larger effort under the Take It Down Act, or TIDA, which aims to tackle the spread of non-consensual AI-generated content.

The case involves a man who was arrested for selling AI-generated deepfakes. What led to his downfall? He used his own photo in his profile. It's almost laughable, but I guess you could say he didn't exactly 'deepfake' his identity well enough. The Take It Down Act, or TIDA, seems to be helping law enforcement make quick work of these cases. Another interesting case involves a woman who was caught sharing AI-generated content featuring a celebrity. Her mistake? She bragged about it on social media, using her own account.

The technology behind AI-generated content, like deepfakes, has advanced rapidly. But here's the thing: while it's getting better at generating realistic images and videos, it's still not perfect. And that's what law enforcement is counting on. When people create and share this content, they often leave behind digital footprints that can be used to track them down. It's a cat-and-mouse game, but it seems law enforcement is currently one step ahead.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
